meta_title: For Students: Claude Code skills setup to Solve no version control (Advanced Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to Claude Code skills setup and overcome no version control. A Advanced-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Claude Code Skills Setup for Version Control
content.seo_description: Master version control as a student. Learn how to leverage Claude Code and Antigravity to automate your git workflows and build professional-grade software.
content.answer_block: To solve the 'no version control' crisis, integrate Claude Code with your local repository. By automating git initialization and committing via CLI-driven AI, you transform messy coding sessions into professional, traceable milestones. This setup ensures every student project is production-ready, version-tracked, and ready for your professional portfolio.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a directory full of 'final_v2_new.py' files. The dread of losing three weeks of progress is real, and the lack of a formal version history is the single biggest barrier between you and a hiring manager’s respect. You aren't just missing a tool; you're missing the backbone of professional software engineering.

But the tide is turning. By mastering Claude Code and Antigravity, you move from a student hacking together scripts to an engineer architecting systems. This isn't just about saving code; it's about building a digital legacy that proves your competency to the world. Let’s turn that chaos into a clean, commit-tracked repository.

steps.title: Initialize the Git Infrastructure
steps.description: Establish the foundation for version control within your project root to enable tracking.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my project directory and generate a comprehensive .gitignore file for my specific tech stack to ensure only source code is tracked.

steps.title: Configure Claude Code Context
steps.description: Link Claude Code to your repository so it understands your project structure and existing code history.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a .claude_context file that outlines my current project architecture and coding standards for AI assistance.

steps.title: Automate Atomic Commits
steps.description: Use Claude Code to analyze your changes and generate professional, descriptive commit messages automatically.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review my recent file changes and draft a semantic commit message following conventional commits standards.

steps.title: Branching Strategy Implementation
steps.description: Create feature branches for new experiments, ensuring the main codebase remains stable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Suggest a feature branch name based on the current ticket I am working on and create it via the CLI.

steps.title: Verifying Integrity and History
steps.description: Perform a final audit of your commit history to ensure your project is ready for a portfolio push.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Audit my git log for the last 10 commits and identify any missing documentation or logical gaps.

pro_tips: Always use atomic commits: one logical change per commit makes debugging 10x faster.
Use Claude Code to write your README.md based on your git commit history to save hours of documentation.
Set up a pre-commit hook that runs Claude Code linting to ensure code quality before every push.
Tag your releases using semantic versioning (v1.0.0) whenever you hit a major project milestone.
If you break a feature, use Claude Code to 'git diff' and compare your current state against a previous stable tag.
content.next_steps: Push your repository to GitHub or GitLab, update your portfolio site with the project link, and start contributing to open-source repositories to practice these skills in a team environment.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical authority to manage professional-grade projects. Stop losing work and start building your reputation. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
